member.office_address: 344 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-1601

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Information provided in this request form may be used or distributed by the Office without prior notice. Information included in this section may be used to help Representative Mann fully explain the request to Kansas-01 on our website or in the media.

fields.label: Enter keywords to search content.
fields.rationale: Standard search term for the relevant agency and account to ensure correct form routing.

fields.label: State
options: Armed Forces (Canada, Europe, Africa, or Middle East)

fields.label: Request Type; Appropriations request typically fall under one of three categories:
options: Funding Requests (Requests a Funding level for an authorized program)
Report Language Requests (requests language to be inserted into the Appropriations Committee Report)
fields.draft_value: Funding Requests (Requests a Funding level for an authorized program)
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request to fund NASA's Science Mission Directorate at a specific levels ($9 billion) for an authorized program.

fields.label: Please give a short title to your request.
fields.draft_value: NASA Science Mission Directorate (SMD) FY 2027 Funding
fields.rationale: This reflects the short title and specific agency account (SMD) identified in the pre-computed reference values.

fields.label: Please list the priority of this request
fields.help_text: If your organization is submitting more than one request, please prioritize this request - 1,2,3, etc.)
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is internal to the submitter's organizational strategy and cannot be determined from the provided context.

fields.label: Problem/Issue Statement (max 250 words).
fields.draft_value:
* Recent budget volatility has destabilized the specialized aerospace workforce and disrupted critical Decadal Survey mission timelines.
* Strategic competitors, particularly China, are rapidly expanding space science capabilities to challenge U.S. leadership in orbital and lunar exploration.
* Sustaining technical excellence requires predictable funding to prevent the loss of high-skill researchers at institutions like Kansas State University and the University of Kansas.
* Current funding levels risk stalling domestic innovation and the development of breakthrough technologies that support national security and economic growth.
fields.rationale: Draws from advocacy talking points regarding China competition, workforce instability, and cites specific local institutions identified in the spending context.

fields.label: Request Description (what will the request do, and why is it necessary?) (Max: 400 words)
fields.draft_value:
* Provides $9 billion for the NASA Science Mission Directorate to support Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences.
* Enables the execution of National Academies' Decadal Survey priorities, ensuring the U.S. remains the global leader in space discovery and planetary defense.
* Supports ongoing research at KS-01 institutions, including Kansas State University's work on global water dynamics and University of Kansas research on atmospheric studies and space defense.
* Ensures the stability of the aerospace talent pipeline by funding 5 active grants in the district (out of 22 total since FY2023), representing over $1.4 million in recent regional investment.
fields.rationale: Incorporates the specific $9B ask, the Decadal Survey justification, and detailed local spending data including grant counts and institutional research topics.

fields.label: Request Goals and Expected Outcomes (Max: 250 words)
fields.draft_value:
* Maintain U.S. preeminence in space science and set international norms for 21st-century exploration ahead of strategic competitors.
* Deliver data-rich outcomes for practical applications, such as improving drought prediction models and tracking global lake and river dynamics.
* Foster a robust STEM workforce in Kansas by supporting foundational science for Moon exploration and advanced aerial radar integration.
* Generate an outsized economic return, estimated at three dollars for every one dollar invested, through industrial innovation and R&D demand.
fields.rationale: Utilizes advocacy talking points on economic ROI and leadership, while citing specific outcomes from active local grants (SWOT dynamics and drought modeling).

fields.label: Which Appropriations Subcommittee does this request fall under?
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs
Transportation, and Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding falls under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee as specified in the reference values and standard congressional procedure.

fields.label: Program name and agency.
fields.draft_value: Science Mission Directorate, National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)
fields.rationale: Matches the account name and agency specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Amount included in FY25 (if applicable)
fields.rationale: The FY2025 enacted amount for NASA Science was $7.334 billion as per the SMD Topline Funding History.

fields.label: Amount included in FY26 (if applicable)
fields.rationale: The FY2026 enacted amount was $7.250 billion, as provided in the Prior Year Enacted reference values.

fields.label: Total amount requested in FY27
fields.rationale: This reflects the specific programmatic funding ask for NASA's Science Mission Directorate provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Bill language request (please include the exact requested language and in what vehicle you would like it to appear - Bill, Report, both)
fields.rationale: Reflects the programmatic funding ask as specified in the reference values; no specific bill/report language was requested beyond the funding level.

fields.label: Which appropriations bill and section do you think best suits your request?
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: Directly from the pre-computed reference values for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Please list other Representatives to whom you are submitting your request (Last name (State-District))
fields.rationale: Identifies the leads of the existing House support letter mentioned in the reference values.

fields.label: Please list other Senators to whom you are submitting your request (Last name (State))
fields.rationale: The reference values state there is no existing support letter for the Senate yet.

fields.label: What organizations are in favor of this request?
fields.draft_value:
* American Astronomical Society (AAS)
* American Geophysical Union (AGU)
fields.rationale: Lists the allied organizations provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Has this, or any similar request, been submitted in prior years (if yes, please specify for what fiscal years)?
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific history question that cannot be answered with provided context.

sections.description: Information included in this section may be used to help Representative Mann fully explain the request to Kansas-01 on our website or in the media.

fields.label: Project Description
fields.draft_value:
* This is a programmatic request for $9 billion to fund NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD).
* SMD conducts scientific exploration enabled by access to space, including Earth observation, planetary discovery, and heliophysics.
* Funding supports a national network of researchers, including 5 active projects in KS-01 focused on water management, drought prediction, and space defense.
* The request ensures the U.S. maintains the technical capabilities and industrial base required to lead the new space race.
fields.rationale: Summarizes the programmatic nature of the request and links it to local KS-01 project counts and strategic goals.

fields.label: Clearly describe the most positive impacts this request will have on the surrounding community, Kansas, and the Nation.
fields.draft_value:
* Supports high-tech research jobs and STEM education at Kansas State University and the University of Kansas Center for Research.
* Provides actionable data for Kansas agriculture through improved drought prediction and river discharge modeling.
* Bolsters national security by funding applied space defense research and maintaining a competitive edge over international rivals like China.
* Drives national economic growth by providing a steady demand for cutting-edge R&D that returns $3 to the economy for every $1 spent.
fields.rationale: Connects national advocacy points (China competition, ROI) to specific local benefits for Kansas (agriculture/drought modeling) and regional institutions.

fields.label: Press Contact Name, Email, Phone Number.

top_grants.description: Water storage in lakes and discharge in rivers are two fundamental components of surface hydrology. Understanding their spatiotemporal dynamics is among swot S primary science goals
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Tracking global lake storage and river discharge dynamics for SWOT.

top_grants.recipient_name: University of Kansas Center for Research Inc
top_grants.description: . This research project aims at studying the challenges and making the required modifications for integrating the snow probing radar onto the vanilla va001 uas  as well as developing the required sub-systems for remote operation of the radar.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing remote operation systems for aerial snow radar integration.

top_grants.recipient_name: University of Kansas Center for Research Inc
top_grants.description: This xrp will use radial velocities from approved and archival programs at NASA/keck'S kpf and nn-explore'S wiyn/neid to measure masses of over 40 sub-neptunes  as well as re-analyzing archival TESS  k2  and Kepler to measure precise planet sizes (via transits) and  where possible  stellar properties (via asteroseismology). Our research program will empirically test the water world hypothesis  and maximizes the scientific return of TESS  k2  and Kepler and ensures that high-priority targets for JWST and ariel are identified and accurately characterized. In addition  our program will keep targets flowing in support of JWST (and future ariel) atmospheric spectroscopy  and along the way it will also quantify stellar activity in planet-hosting systems across a range of stellar types that closely tracks the hwo target distribution  measure system architectures  and constrain interior compositions of planets straddling the super-earth/sub-neptune boundary.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Characterizing sub-Neptune planets for JWST atmospheric studies.

top_grants.recipient_name: University of Kansas Center for Research Inc
top_grants.description: The goal of this proposal is to provide an observationally based understanding of the role of land-atmosphere (L-A) coupling in the evolution of drought and replicate these processes in remote sensing based forecast model
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Modeling land-air interactions to predict and track evolving droughts.

top_grants.recipient_name: University of Kansas Center for Research Inc
top_grants.description: The roses funding opportunity goals are: execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress. Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond. Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Supporting foundational science for Moon exploration and applied space defense.

top_grant_recipients.name: University of Kansas Center for Research Inc
